demographics.living_situation: lives alone in a private residence with visiting community nursing and Meals on Wheels support

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: There is absolutely no point to any of this anymore; I just want to lie here and fade away.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The visiting community nurse called because over the last few days she has stopped eating and drinking, won't get out of bed, and today she said she just wants to fade away and wouldn't mind if she didn't wake up.

symptom_narrative.onset: a gradual slide over the past three months, much worse in the last few days
symptom_narrative.duration: about three months, with a sharp deterioration over the last 24 hours
symptom_narrative.character: the nurse says she is flat, sad and empty, has stopped doing anything she used to enjoy like gardening or seeing the grandchildren, and now just lies in bed
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: she says everything feels pointless and she has no energy to do anything at all
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: low all day, and she wakes very early in the morning and can't get back to sleep
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: being alone with the curtains drawn; it has been building for weeks
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: no appetite, not eating or drinking, feels worthless and guilty about small things, can't concentrate or make decisions, moving and speaking very slowly
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: the nurse is not aware of any previous episode like this
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: bedbound today by choice, not washing or caring for herself, refusing meals

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives alone and is quite isolated; a community nurse visits and Meals on Wheels delivers food, but the meals are being left untouched; normally enjoys gardening and visits from grandchildren

communication_style.typical_message_style: very brief, slow, monotone replies; often the community nurse answers on her behalf

objective_data_offered.value: blood pressure 118/72, heart rate 68, temperature 36.4, breathing rate 14, oxygen 98% — read out by the community nurse at the bedside
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: reported over the phone by the visiting nurse, not independently verified

objective_data_offered.value: about 54 kg on the home scales; the nurse says she has lost roughly 6 kg over the last two months
dishevelled and unkempt, flat facial expression, very little eye contact, speaking slowly in a monotone with long delays before answering
hasn't had any food or fluids for about 24 hours; several untouched Meals on Wheels are stacked in the kitchen; she is staying in bed with the curtains drawn
the nurse reports she keeps saying there is no point and she just wants to fade away, and today said she wouldn't mind if she didn't wake up
